title: Default constructors and default arguments

exercises.title: Inventory Item with Default and Parameterized Constructors
exercises.prompt_md:
## Goal
Complete the `Item` class so it can be constructed with no arguments, with only a name, or with both a name and a quantity.

## Input
- Line 1: integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 20), the number of items to read.
- Lines 2..N+1: each line contains one of:
  - the single word `DEFAULT` (construct with no arguments),
  - a single string with no spaces (construct with name only, quantity defaults to 1),
  - a string followed by a space and an integer (construct with name and quantity).

## Output
For each item, print one line in the format `name: qty` where `name` is the item's name and `qty` is its quantity.
- When constructed with no arguments, name must be `unknown` and quantity must be `0`.
- When constructed with name only, quantity must be `1`.

## Requirements
1. Define `Item` with private members: `std::string m_name` (in-class initializer `"unknown"`) and `int m_qty` (in-class initializer `0`).
2. Define `Item() = default` which uses the in-class initializers (name="unknown", qty=0).
3. Define `Item(const std::string& name, int qty = 1)` using a member initializer list for the parameterized form.
4. Provide `const std::string& getName() const` and `int getQty() const` accessors.
5. Input lines containing `DEFAULT` must use zero-argument construction (`Item{}`), producing `unknown: 0`.
6. For non-DEFAULT lines, read the entire line with `std::getline`, then parse it: if it contains a space, extract the name and quantity; otherwise use the whole line as the name (qty defaults to 1).
7. Output lines use the exact format `name: qty` with no extra spaces.

## Notes
- Use `std::string`, `std::getline`, and `std::stoi` for robust line-based parsing.
- After reading N with `std::cin >> n`, consume the trailing newline with `std::cin.ignore()` before the `std::getline` loop.

test_cases.label: Hidden: name-only and full construction edge cases

exercises.title: Temperature Reading with Default-Argument Constructor
exercises.prompt_md:
## Goal
Complete the `TempReading` class and write a program that reads a sequence of temperature readings and prints a summary using each object's member functions.

## Input
- Line 1: integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 50), the number of readings.
- Lines 2..N+1: each line is one of:
  - `DEFAULT` — use a zero-argument construction (sensor id = 0, celsius = 20.0).
  - a single integer S — construction with sensor id S only (celsius defaults to 20.0).
  - an integer S followed by a double T — construction with sensor id S and celsius T.

## Output
For each reading, print one line:
`Sensor S: T C` where S is the sensor id and T is the celsius value printed to **one decimal place**.

Example line: `Sensor 3: 36.6 C`

## Requirements
1. Define a **single constructor** `TempReading(int sensorId = 0, double celsius = 20.0)` using a member initializer list. This one constructor handles all three construction forms — do **not** use `= default` or a separate no-argument overload.
2. Confirm that `TempReading{}` compiles and produces sensorId = 0, celsius = 20.0 (because all parameters have defaults, this constructor *is* the default constructor).
3. Provide `int getSensorId() const` and `double getCelsius() const` member functions.
4. Output uses `std::fixed` and `std::setprecision(1)`.
5. When a line contains only an integer (no double following it), celsius must default to `20.0`. When the line is `DEFAULT`, both must use their defaults (0 and 20.0).
6. Use `std::getline` to read each line, then parse it with a `std::istringstream`.
7. Hidden tests include: all `DEFAULT` lines, all lines with negative temperatures, and a single reading with N = 1.

## Notes
- Include `<sstream>` for `std::istringstream`.
- After reading N with `std::cin >> n`, consume the trailing newline with `std::cin.ignore()` before the `std::getline` loop.

test_cases.label: Sample: mixed input forms
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Sensor 0: 20.0 C
Sensor 3: 20.0 C
Sensor 7: 36.6 C
Sensor 1: -10.0 C
Sensor 2: 20.0 C

test_cases.label: Hidden: all DEFAULT, N=1 edge case
Hidden: negative temps and large sensor ids
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Sensor 100: -273.1 C
Sensor 0: 20.0 C
Sensor 42: 0.0 C
Sensor 99: 20.0 C
